html:
Exclusive to:

WerepigHound

Suspicious Moonrocks are naturally spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. They are Moon Rock-made sculptures shaped like Werepigs and Hounds and can be found scattered around the Moon Stone set piece. Mining them with a Pickaxe will yield one Moon Rock plus 50% to get one extra.

Additionally, after placing a Star Caller's Staff onto the Moon Stone during a Full Moon, several Werepigs and Hounds will spawn and attack the Staff. After breaking the base, all of them will turn into Suspicious Moonrock sculptures. They will turn once again into the corresponding Mobs if the base is fixed and the staff is put back on it.

There are three different appearances for the Werepig, corresponding to its attack-, death and howl-animations, and two different appearances for the Hound, corresponding to its attack- and death-animations.

Tips

  • One should mine down all Suspicious Moonrocks before attempting to use the Moon Stone again, as it will keep spawning more enemies along with the ones from previous attempts.

Trivia

  • Suspicious Moonrocks were introduced in the A Little Fixer Upper update.

Gallery

  • The Moon Stone set piece featuring several Suspicious Moonrocks.
  • The three different appearances of the Moonrock Werepig.
  • The two different appearances of the Moonrock Hound.
  • A petrified Werepig next to the Moon Stone.
  • All three Werepig types.
  • Both Hound types.
  • A Werepig Moonrock, as seen in the A New Reign: Part 1 trailer.
  • Suspicious Moonrock sculptures as seen in the A New Reign trailer.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

WerepigHound
“It looks very lifelike.”
–Wilson, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“At least it doesn't smell anymore.”
–Willow, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Wolfgang does not like that.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“I can make that face too...”
–Wendy, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“IT IS BETTER THIS WAY”
–WX-78, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Lunar light catalyzes a strange reaction in its skin.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Hmph. Maybe I don't got it so bad.”
–Woodie, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Not so tough.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Dö nöt cöwer behind your carapace of röck! Fight!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Yikes.”
–Webber, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“It won't begin to move, will it?”
–Warly, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Arrrooooo!”
–Wormwood, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“At least it's not trying to kill us now.”
–Winona, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Let's not be here when that wakes up!”
–Wortox, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Glurph. Don't like having this around.”
–Wurt, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Don't worry Woby, it's just a statue.”
–Walter, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“Something's nagging at the back of my brain... I'm sure it's nothing.”
–Wanda, when examining a Petrified Werepig.
“I don't like how it's looking at me.”
–Wilson, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Lookit this big dumb lawn ornament!”
–Willow, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Is make Wolfgang uncomfortable!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“A "puppy", frozen in time...”
–Wendy, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“A MAJOR IMPROVEMENT”
–WX-78, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Completely petrified as a result of lunar exposure.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“That's unfortunate there, buddy.”
–Woodie, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Try and get me now. Ha!”
–Maxwell, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Shed yöur stöne förm and fight, beast!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Haha. We can stack things on its head and it won't even move.”
–Webber, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“I feel strangely uneasy.”
–Warly, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Just stone”
–Wormwood, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Something scare ya? You look petrified!”
–Winona, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“I hope that hellhound's in on the prank.”
–Wortox, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“It look surprised.”
–Wurt, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“Wouldn't it be funny if it came to life?”
–Walter, when examining a Petrified Hound.
“The attention to detail is incredible!”
–Wanda, when examining a Petrified Hound.
Suspicious Moonrocks are naturally spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. They are Moon Rock-made sculptures shaped like Werepigs and Hounds and can be found scattered around the Moon Stone set piece. Mining them with a Pickaxe will yield one Moon Rock plus 50% to get one extra.
Additionally, after placing a Star Caller's Staff onto the Moon Stone during a Full Moon, several Werepigs and Hounds will spawn and attack the Staff. After breaking the base, all of them will turn into Suspicious Moonrock sculptures. They will turn once again into the corresponding Mobs if the base is fixed and the staff is put back on it.
There are three different appearances for the Werepig, corresponding to its attack-, death and howl-animations, and two different appearances for the Hound, corresponding to its attack- and death-animations.
Tips[edit | edit source]
One should mine down all Suspicious Moonrocks before attempting to use the Moon Stone again, as it will keep spawning more enemies along with the ones from previous attempts.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Suspicious Moonrocks were introduced in the A Little Fixer Upper update.
